summary: Patrick announces a new feature for semi-automatically generating monthly sales reports for Bingo Card Creator. He explains that while the more impressive backend features can't be shown publicly due to customer data integration, he has made the monthly sales report completely anonymized. He emphasizes his paranoid approach to customer data privacy, noting the backend code isn't even on the production server.
key_points: Built semi-automatic monthly sales report generation for radical transparency
Customer data privacy treated as paramount — backend analysis code kept off production servers entirely
Public sales stats shared openly as a form of transparent business reporting
money_quotes: It is physically impossible for anyone to get from the sales data to any identifiable information about customers, which would be 101 flavors of bad.
The actual backend code is not even uploaded on the production copy of the server. I'm a little paranoid, what can I say.
key_insight: Sharing business metrics publicly builds trust and community, but customer data privacy must be treated as an absolute constraint rather than a tradeoff.
